name: Throw
description: Throwis a commonActionwhich allows the user to throw certainWeapons,Consumables, environmental objects, and even other creatures.DescriptionThrow a character or item from the world or your inventory.YourStrengthaffects how much weight you can throw. Heavier items deal more damage.The damage of weapons with thethrownproperty is the same as the weapon's melee damage.PropertiesCost:ActionDamage:Normal weapon damageDetails:Attack RollRange: 3-18 m (10-60 ft)The range depends on the weight of the object, see below. Range for picking up objects from the ground isAoE: 1 m (3 ft) RadiusDamage:For weapons with theThrownproperty, the damage and damage type are the same as the weapon's melee damage.For other objects and creatures, damage is determined by the weight[1]of the object being thrown:Light objects, underWeight: 10 kg (20 lb):1⁠⁠BludgeoningdamageMedium objects,Weight: 10 kg (20 lb) - 50 kg / 100 lb:1d4⁠⁠BludgeoningdamageHeavy objects, overWeight: 50 kg (100 lb):2d4⁠⁠BludgeoningdamageIf the thrown object or creature is over half the target's weight, the target is knocked back 2 m (7 ft).[1]If the thrown object or creature is heavier than the target, the target becomesProne(nosaving throw).[1]A thrown creature always landsProne(nosaving throw).Throwing a creature requires a successfulAthleticscheck, using the same DC as whenshoving a creature, except the check is not made at advantage when hidden.RangeThe range of the Throw attack depends on the object's weight in kilograms and the thrower's Strength ability, according to the following formula:[2]round(3+15⋅15⋅Strength2−Weight2⋅Weight)The formula's output is adjusted to 3 meters if it would have been lower and 18 meters if it would have been higher.
